  • DDT

DDT¶

The ANF of an n variables Boolean function is a polynomial with n variables and coefficients in GF(2) The ANF of a vectorial Boolean (n,m)-function is a sequence of m ANFs of n variables Boolean functions The univariate representation of an n variables Boolean function is an univariate polynomial with coefficients in GF(2^n) such that its image is in GF(2) The univariate representation of a vectorial Boolean (n,n)-function is an univariate polynomial with coefficients in GF(2^n)

IntToSequence, IntToElt, getConvertionFunction

 1TruthTableToMatrixDDT:=function(TT,n,m)
 2    DDT:=ZeroMatrix(Integers(), 2^n, 2^m);
 3    r:=2^n;
 4    DDT[1][1]:=2^n;
 5    for i:=1 to (r-1) do
 6        for x:=0 to (r-1) do
 7            DDT[i+1][BitwiseXor(TT[x+1],TT[BitwiseXor(x, i)+1 ])+1]+:=1;
 8        end for;
 9    end for;
10    return DDT;
11end function;
12
13.. code-block:: text
14:linenos:
15
16DDT:=function(F:  anf:=false)
17    if anf then
18        m:=#F;
19        n:=Rank(Parent(F[1]));
20        D:=getConvertionFunction(n: vector:=true);
21        if m eq n then
22            cD:=D;
23        else
24            cD:=getConvertionFunction(m : vector:=true);
25        end if;
26        P:=PowerSequence(Integers());
27        TT:=[ Seqint(P!([Evaluate(F[j],D[i]): j in [1..m]]),2): i in [0..(2^n-1)] ];
28    else
29        K:=BaseRing(Parent(F));
30        m:=Degree(K);
31        n:=m;
32        D:=getConvertionFunction(n);
33        cD:=D;
34        P:=PowerSequence(Integers());
35        TT:=[ Seqint(P!Eltseq(Evaluate(F,D[i])),2): i in [0..(2^n-1)]];
36    end if;
37    mDDT:=TruthTableToMatrixDDT(TT,n,m);
38    DDT:=AssociativeArray();
39    for i:=0 to (2^n-1) do
40        Di:=D[i];
41        DDT[Di]:=AssociativeArray();
42        for j:=0 to (2^m-1) do
43            DDT[Di][cD[j]]:=mDDT[i+1][j+1];
44        end for;
45    end for;
46    return DDT;
47end function;
